title: A phototoxic drug reaction due to topical NSAIDs
case_prompt: A 52Гўв‚¬ВђyearГўв‚¬Вђold man with AddisonГўв‚¬в„ўs disease on lifelong corticosteroid replacement and a history of lateral epicondylitis presented with a 7Гўв‚¬Вђday history of severe redness around his right elbow accompanied by intense burning and stinging. On examination, there was a bright red, edematous, crusted erythema over the right elbow and a maculopapular rash on the trunk. title: Recurrent Ameloblastic Fibroma: Report of a Rare Case
case_prompt:
An 18-year-old woman presented with a 1-year history of slowly enlarging gingival overgrowth in the left posterior mandible that interfered with chewing but was painless. Intraoral examination revealed a 3ГѓвЂ”4 cm exophytic mass extending from the left mandibular second molar to the retromolar pad, buccally into the vestibule and inferiorly to the floor of the mouth. Panoramic radiograph showed a well-defined radiolucency around the impacted left third molar. The lesion and the impacted tooth were excised en bloc. 

Grossly, the specimen included both intraosseous and extraosseous components. Histologic examination demonstrated cords, interconnecting strands, and islands of odontogenic epithelium embedded in a cell-rich, myxoid mesenchymal stroma. The epithelial strands and cords were lined by a double layer of cuboidal cells. The islands exhibited peripheral tall columnar cells with polarized nuclei and clear, vacuolated cytoplasm surrounding central stellate reticulum-like cells. Juxtaepithelial hyalinization was noted around some islands. No hard-tissue (enamel or dentin) formation was seen. The cellularity varied, with focal hypercellular areas and other sparsely cellular, myxoid regions. A thin fibrous capsule partially surrounded the lesion. No cytologic atypia or mitotic figures were observed on multiple sections.
diagnostic_reasoning: 1. Ameloblastoma Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“AF needs to be differentiated from ameloblastomaГўв‚¬В¦Гўв‚¬Вќ 2. Odontogenic myxoma Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“AF needs to be differentiated from Гўв‚¬В¦ odontogenic myxomaГўв‚¬В¦Гўв‚¬Вќ 3. Dentigerous cyst Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“AF needs to be differentiated from Гўв‚¬В¦ dentigerous cystГўв‚¬В¦Гўв‚¬Вќ 4. Odontogenic keratocyst Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“AF needs to be differentiated from Гўв‚¬В¦ odontogenic keratocystГўв‚¬В¦Гўв‚¬Вќ 5. Central giant cell granuloma Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“AF needs to be differentiated from Гўв‚¬В¦ central giant cell granulomaГўв‚¬В¦Гўв‚¬Вќ 6. Histiocytosis Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“AF needs to be differentiated from Гўв‚¬В¦ histiocytosis.Гўв‚¬Вќ 7. Ameloblastic fibroodontoma/fibrodentinoma Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“There was no hard tissue formation, thus eliminating ameloblastic fibroodontoma/fibrodentinoma.Гўв‚¬Вќ 8. Ameloblastic fibrosarcoma Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“Presence of numerous mitotic cells or any atypical mitosis should suggest malignant entities such as AFS in the differential diagnosisГўв‚¬В¦ atypia and mitotic activities were not evident.Гўв‚¬Вќ

title:
Refractory scleroderma renal crisis precipitated after high-dose oral
corticosteroids and concurrent intravitreal injection of
bevacizumab
case_prompt:
A 37-year-old man presented with a 3-month history of progressive skin thickening, initially on his torso and then spreading diffusely, accompanied by a 20Гўв‚¬вЂњ30-lb weight loss and fatigue. He denied RaynaudГўв‚¬в„ўs phenomenon, dyspnea, or wheezing. His blood pressure at presentation was 100Гўв‚¬вЂњ110вЂ‰mmHg systolic, with a serum creatinine of 0.8вЂ‰mg/dL. He had a history of treated hepatitis B without active disease.

Serologic studies showed a negative antinuclear antibody, negative antiГўв‚¬вЂњSmith and antiГўв‚¬вЂњribonucleoprotein antibodies, and low-level antiГўв‚¬вЂњtopoisomerase I (3Гўв‚¬вЂњ4вЂ‰AU/mL). Nailfold capillaroscopy was suggestive of systemic sclerosis, and a skin biopsy was read as suspicious for morphea versus systemic sclerosis. Echocardiography revealed no pulmonary hypertension or pericardial effusion. He was started on mycophenolate mofetil.

An IgG ГЋВ» monoclonal protein of 1.1вЂ‰g/dL was detected. Bone marrow biopsy showed 10% ГЋВ»-restricted plasma cells without high-risk cytogenetics besides 1q and 5q gains, monosomy 13, and 14q deletions. Three months after presentation, for unclear reasons, he was started on high-dose prednisone (60вЂ‰mg daily). Shortly thereafter, his systolic blood pressure increased to 140Гўв‚¬вЂњ150вЂ‰mmHg and serum creatinine rose to 1.1вЂ‰mg/dL. He developed blurry vision; ophthalmologic examination revealed cotton-wool spots. He received two intravitreal injections of bevacizumab (1.25вЂ‰mg each).

One week after the injections, he was admitted with severe hypertension (systolic blood pressures 200Гўв‚¬вЂњ220вЂ‰mmHg), a rise in serum creatinine to 1.4вЂ‰mg/dL, and new proteinuria (urine protein-creatinine ratio 1вЂ‰g/g). Renal ultrasound with Doppler showed normal-sized kidneys and no evidence of renal artery stenosis. Given the abrupt hypertension, worsening renal function, proteinuria, recent corticosteroid exposure, and intravitreal VEGF blockade, scleroderma renal crisis was suspected, and a complement-mediated thrombotic microangiopathy related to VEGF inhibition could not be ruled out. A renal biopsy was planned after blood pressure control.
diagnostic_reasoning: 1. Scleroderma renal crisis was the leading diagnosis given malignant hypertension and acute kidney injury in a patient with systemic sclerosis after corticosteroid exposure Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“Given the sudden change in blood pressure, renal function, proteinuria, and recent exposure to oral corticosteroids, SRC was suspected.Гўв‚¬Вќ 2. VEGF-blockadeГўв‚¬вЂњinduced thrombotic microangiopathy remained in the differential because intravitreal bevacizumab can cause endothelial injury and TMA Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“A complement-mediated TMA related to intravitreal VEGF inhibition could also not be ruled out.Гўв‚¬Вќ

title: Gallbladder volvulus in a 5Гўв‚¬вЂњyears old Vietnamese female: AВ case report
case_prompt:
A previously healthy 5Гўв‚¬ВђyearГўв‚¬Вђold girl presented with 9 hours of intermittent, moderateГўв‚¬вЂњseverity epigastric pain radiating to the right lower quadrant. The pain was unchanged by position and was associated with multiple episodes of nonbloody vomiting. She was afebrile, had normal urination and bowel movements, and reported a similar, selfГўв‚¬Вђlimited episode 1 month earlier.

On examination, she was alert, without signs of systemic infection. Abdominal palpation elicited tenderness in the epigastrium; there was no guarding or rebound. MurphyГўв‚¬в„ўs sign was positive, and there was no jaundice.

Laboratory studies showed normal hepatic and biliary function tests and an elevated C-reactive protein level of 30.2 mg/L. A supine abdominal radiograph was unremarkable.

On repeat ultrasound 24 hours later, the gallbladder measured 53 ГѓвЂ” 33 mm with a 3.1 mm wall, lacked vascular flow, contained biliary sludge, and showed a cone-shaped hypoechoic structure at the neck; the fundus was displaced to the left of its fossa and moved with patient repositioning.

Contrast-enhanced CT of the abdomen revealed a 53.5 ГѓвЂ” 22.8 ГѓвЂ” 31.5 mm gallbladder with an irregular, poorly enhancing wall, an intraluminal hyperdense area suggestive of hemorrhage, a 3 mm hyperdense nodule, fundus deviation to the left of the gallbladder bed, pericholecystic fluid and fat stranding, and focal hepatic perfusion abnormalities.

Gallbladder volvulus was diagnosed preoperatively due to the ultrasound finding of Гўв‚¬Е“a cone shaped hypoechogenic structure Гўв‚¬В¦ at the neck of the gallbladder which was a suggestive sign of acute GVГўв‚¬Вќ and CT findings of Гўв‚¬Е“poorly enhanced gallbladder wall after contrastГўв‚¬Вќ with Гўв‚¬Е“fundus Гўв‚¬В¦ deviated to the left Гўв‚¬В¦ evocative of GV.Гўв‚¬Вќ

title: Bullous fixed drug eruption following administration of the recombinant adjuvant Shingrix vaccine

title: Rapidly progressive central retinal pigmented epithelium atrophy after Ivospemin (SBP-101) treatment for pancreatic adenocarcinoma
journal: American Journal of Ophthalmology Case Reports
case_prompt: A 69-year-old woman with metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (peritoneal and hepatic metastases) enrolled in a trial of SBP-101 combined with nab-paclitaxel and gemcitabine presented with a 6-month history of progressive blurred vision and visual field loss. She had no diabetes or hypertension. Baseline ophthalmologic evaluation before SBP-101 showed 20/20 visual acuity (VA) in both eyes, normal intraocular pressures, and normal macular structure on optical coherence tomography (OCT). After the second monthly cycle of SBP-101, her VA fell to 20/50 in the right eye and 20/40 in the left eye. Fundus examination revealed pigmentary changes in the macula, with punctate hypo- and hyperautofluorescent spots on blue-autofluorescence. OCT demonstrated ellipsoid zone disruption and hyperreflective material between the Bruch membrane and retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) in both eyes. SBP-101 was discontinued, and intravitreal dexamethasone was administered bilaterally without improvement. One month later, VA was 20/50 OD and 20/32 OS. Full-field electroretinography (ERG) showed normal scotopic responses and photopic amplitudes at the lower limit of normal. Serum anti-recoverin and anti-enolase autoantibodies were within normal limits. Over the next five months, VA declined to 20/5000 OD and 20/4000 OS, with photopsias and severely impaired central vision. UltraГўв‚¬вЂњwide-field fundus photographs revealed bilateral roundish macular areas of irregular pigment loss extending beyond the vascular arcades, with scattered dot-blot hemorrhages. Blue-autofluorescence showed central hypoautofluorescence surrounded by a ring of alternating hyper- and hypoautofluorescent dots. OCT of the macula demonstrated incomplete RPE and outer retinal atrophy with signal hypertransmission to the choroid, ellipsoid zone disruption, and perifoveal hyperreflective sub-RPE deposits; choroidal thickness was reduced. Optic nerve OCT and RNFL thickness were normal. Visual fields showed bilateral central relative scotomas (VFI 26% OD, 31% OS). ERG per ISCEV standards revealed preserved dark-adapted 0.01 cdГ‚В·s/m2 and mixed rodГўв‚¬вЂњcone responses, relatively preserved a-waves on light-adapted single flashes, but reduced amplitudes and slightly prolonged implicit times on 30 Hz flicker.
diagnostic_reasoning: 1. Cisplatin-induced toxicity was reviewed since cisplatin can cause cone dysfunction and ischemic retinal changes Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“cisplatin induces a dose-dependent damage photoreceptor cells, presenting in the form of cone malfunction, macular pigmentary changes and minor retinal ischemia changesГўв‚¬Вќ Гўв‚¬вЂќ yet was deemed unlikely as the patient had not received cisplatin. 2. Cancer-associated retinopathy (CAR) was evaluated because CAR can present with retinal atrophy and ERG abnormalities Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“CAR Гўв‚¬В¦ initial phases show a normal fundus Гўв‚¬В¦ successively retinal atrophy Гўв‚¬В¦ may appearГўв‚¬Вќ Гўв‚¬вЂќ but was ruled out as Гўв‚¬Е“scotopic responses [were] within the limits,Гўв‚¬Вќ photopic changes were progressive but localized, and Гўв‚¬Е“anti-enolase and anti-recoverin [were] Гўв‚¬В¦ within the limits.Гўв‚¬Вќ

title: Seizure and coma secondary to ConnГўв‚¬в„ўs syndrome: a case report
case_prompt: A 48-year-old man of African descent was brought to the emergency department unresponsive after a generalized tonicГўв‚¬вЂњclonic seizure. On examination he was comatose with a blood pressure of 178/121 mmHg, heart rate 105 beats/min, respiratory rate 18 breaths/min, and a Glasgow Coma Scale score of 3 of 15; he required intubation and ICU admission. He had no history of epilepsy, head trauma, recreational drug use, smoking, or alcohol consumption. His medical history was notable for resistant hypertension diagnosed 20 years ago, treated with atenolol 50 mg, spironolactone 100 mg, amlodipine 10 mg, and valsartan 160 mg daily, with poor compliance, and for type II diabetes mellitus treated with a sulfonylurea. Family history was positive for hypertension but negative for renal disease or adrenal tumors. Body mass index was 31. Thyroid and visual-field examinations were normal, and there were no cushingoid or acromegaly features. Initial laboratory studies showed a potassium level of 2.6 mmol/L (normal 3.6Гўв‚¬вЂњ5.1), creatinine 113 ГЋВјmol/L, metabolic alkalosis on blood gas analysis, urea 5.7 mmol/L, and creatine kinase 2.2 ГЋВјg/L. Urinalysis was negative for leukocyte esterase and nitrites. A chest radiograph showed no signs of heart failure. ECG demonstrated sinus rhythm. Troponin-T and BNP were within normal limits. Repeat potassium remained low at 2.6 mmol/L; plasma aldosterone was 36.2 ng/dL (normal 2.8Гўв‚¬вЂњ15.8), and plasma renin was < 0.081 ng/dL (normal 0.4Гўв‚¬вЂњ2.3). A 24-hour urine collection excluded CushingГўв‚¬в„ўs syndrome and pheochromocytoma. Renal ultrasound and cardiac echocardiography excluded renovascular and cardiac causes of secondary hypertension.

Cardiac arrhythmia (atrial fibrillation) ruled out Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“Cardiovascular risk factors leading to neurological manifestations, such as atrial fibrillation, were ruled out by a normal ECG.Гўв‚¬Вќ 4. Uremic encephalopathy excluded Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“Renal failure and uremic encephalopathy were excluded by normal kidney function parameters.Гўв‚¬Вќ 5. Renovascular and cardiac causes of secondary hypertension excluded Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“Renal ultrasound and cardiac echography results excluded the presence of renovascular and cardiac causes for secondary hypertension.Гўв‚¬Вќ

title: Sclerosing meningioma with a large peritumoral cyst: Case report
case_prompt: A 53-year-old man with no significant medical history presented with a headache. Neurologic examination revealed that he was alert and had no motor or sensory deficits, but he noted narrowing of the left lower visual field quadrant. Noncontrast CT of the head showed a 5-cm, well-circumscribed, high-density mass in the right parietal region without calcification. MRI demonstrated an extra-axial lesion that was isoГўв‚¬Вђintense on T1-weighted imaging, uniformly very low in signal on T2-weighted imaging, and showed marked enhancement after gadolinium administration. There was no diffusion restriction after correction for a T2 blackout effect. The mass was separate from the ventricles and was associated with a large anterior cyst whose contents had a signal different from cerebrospinal fluid; the cyst wall did not enhance, consistent with a peritumoral cyst, and there was surrounding edema. The tumor contacted the dura mater convexity and the falx cerebri but lacked an obvious attachment or dural tail sign. Time-resolved MR angiography (4D-TRAK) showed no tumor staining or feeding vessels, suggesting low vascularity. Routine blood tests, including tumor markers, were unremarkable. Contrast-enhanced CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis revealed no extracranial source of malignancy.
diagnostic_reasoning: 1. Metastatic tumor was considered but ruled out Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“contrastГўв‚¬Вђenhanced CT of the trunk showed no mass lesions that could be the primary tumor.Гўв‚¬Вќ

title: Neglected adult PFAPA syndrome
case_prompt:

Each episode featured a temperature of 38.5Гўв‚¬вЂњ40 Г‚В°C lasting up to 7 days, accompanied by sore throat, cervical and submandibular lymphadenopathy and pain, headache, myalgia, fatigue, and dysphagia. PET-CT demonstrated increased glucose metabolism in the sigmoid colon, but colonoscopy was normal. Pharyngoscopy revealed pharyngitis with white exudate. Ultrasound showed enlarged bilateral cervical, submandibular, and supraclavicular lymph nodes (up to 21 ГѓвЂ” 6 mm), and a supraclavicular lymph node biopsy revealed reactive hyperplasia. Bone marrow biopsy and cytogenetics were unremarkable. Despite multiple courses of antibiotics, febrile episodes persisted.

No abnormalities were found in the bacterial cultures of the throat and blood samples.Гўв‚¬Вќ 2. Neoplastic disease excluded Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“PET-CT Indicated increased glucose metabolism in the sigmoid colon Гўв‚¬В¦ while the colonoscopy results were normal. Гўв‚¬В¦ lymph node biopsy pathology indicated reactive lymph node hyperplasia.Гўв‚¬Вќ 3. Hyper IgD syndrome excluded Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“High IgD levels and decreased mevalonate kinase activity support the diagnosis of HIDS.Гўв‚¬Вќ 4. PFAPA syndrome supported Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“Periodic fever with cervical lymphadenitis and pharyngitis. Inflammatory markers are elevated during fever attacks and without any symptoms during the febrile intervals. Throat swab cultures performed with fever was negative and not responsive to antibiotic therapy. Infectious, neoplastic diseases, monogenic and febrile polygenic auto inflammatory diseases (AIDs) were excluded.Гўв‚¬Вќ

title: Rhabdomyolysis as cause, consequence, or mimicker of myocardial infarction: A case report
case_prompt:
A 65-year-old man was brought to the emergency department because of progressive fatigue, diffuse myalgia, and altered mental status. His medical history included chronic ischemic heart disease status post coronary-artery bypass grafting, hypertension, diabetes mellitus, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, and stage 3 chronic kidney disease.  

On arrival, he reported several days of worsening leg and shoulder pain with an inability to walk independently. Vital signs showed hypotension, and an electrocardiogram revealed atrial fibrillation, a left bundle-branch block, ST-segment depression, and peaked, high T waves. Laboratory tests demonstrated a troponin T level of 532вЂ‰ng/L (normal <вЂ‰14вЂ‰ng/L) and NT-proBNP of 5579вЂ‰ng/L (normal <вЂ‰125вЂ‰ng/L). A temporary pacemaker was placed because of bradyarrhythmia.  

Urgent coronary angiography showed severe three-vessel atheromatous disease with an 80% stenosis in the left anterior descending artery and sub-occlusive lesions in the right coronary artery, which was successfully stented. During the procedure, he developed cardiogenic shock, treated with vasopressors and inotropic support. Arterial blood gas analysis disclosed a metabolic acidosis (pH 7.26) and a serum potassium concentration of 7.5вЂ‰mmol/L.  

He was admitted to the intensive care unit. Further laboratory results included a creatine phosphokinase level >вЂ‰22,000вЂ‰U/L (normal 39Гўв‚¬вЂњ308вЂ‰U/L), a myoglobin level >вЂ‰30,000вЂ‰ГЋВјg/L (normal <вЂ‰72вЂ‰ГЋВјg/L), aspartate aminotransferase 1406вЂ‰U/L (normal <вЂ‰50вЂ‰U/L), lactate dehydrogenase 1969вЂ‰U/L (normal 50Гўв‚¬вЂњ250вЂ‰U/L), blood urea nitrogen 304вЂ‰mg/dL (normal 10Гўв‚¬вЂњ71вЂ‰mg/dL), and serum creatinine 6.58вЂ‰mg/dL (normal 0.67Гўв‚¬вЂњ1.17вЂ‰mg/dL).
diagnostic_reasoning: 1. Initial suspicion of myocardial infarction Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“On arrival Гўв‚¬В¦ electrocardiogram revealed Гўв‚¬В¦ ST-segment depression and peaked, high T wavesГўв‚¬Вќ 2. Hyperkalaemia can mimic ischemic EKG changes Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“potassium disorders can cause EKG alterations including peaked T waves and bradyarrhythmia, present in our patientГўв‚¬в„ўs EKGГўв‚¬Вќ 3. MI-associated rhabdomyolysis after defibrillation is unlikely here Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“Association between MI and rhabdomyolysis Гўв‚¬В¦ usually occurring after cardiac arrest, CPR, and defibrillationГўв‚¬Вќ

title: Ice Pack Test Eased Ptosis in a Patient Presenting with a Possible Oculomotor Nerve Schwannoma: A Case Report
case_prompt:
A 32-year-old man presented with excessive tearing of the left eye and mild ocular pain. Over the previous 4Гўв‚¬вЂњ5 months he had noticed progressive left-eyelid drooping and intermittent diplopia. He reported a mild left-sided head trauma 1.5 years earlier but had no other medical history or family history of neurological or autoimmune disease.

On ophthalmologic examination, visual acuity, intraocular pressure, and funduscopy were normal. The left eyelid was ptotic, and there was limitation of adduction, elevation, and depression of the left eye, with a dilated left pupil. No other neurological deficits were found. Fluorescein angiography, indocyanine green chorioangiography, and optical coherence tomography were unremarkable. The patient noted that his ptosis fluctuated during the day, with marked improvement when washing his face with cold water.

To evaluate for myasthenia gravis, an ice pack was applied to the left eyelid for 2 minutes, after which his ptosis improved significantly, with near-complete eyelid opening; the improvement lasted only a few minutes.
diagnostic_reasoning: 1. Myasthenia gravis was considered because of fluctuating ptosis and cold-induced improvement Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“Interestingly, the patient reported fluctuation of his left-eyelid droop during daytime and significant improvement when washing his face with cold water.Гўв‚¬Вќ 2. Pupillary involvement argued against MG since ocular MG does not affect pupils Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“ocular MG is characterized by diplopia and/or ptosis, whereas pupillary function is always normal.Гўв‚¬Вќ

title: Mycoplasma pneumoniaeГўв‚¬Вђassociated encephalitis complicated by cerebral salt wasting syndrome
case_prompt:
An 8-year-old girl was brought to the emergency department with reduced consciousness and an 8-day history of high fever, accompanied by vomiting, epigastric pain, and a mild cough. On arrival, her pulse was 112/min, respiratory rate 19/min, blood pressure 93/62 mmвЂ‰Hg, and temperature 36 Г‚В°C. Physical examination was unremarkable. title: Panic Attack, Severe Hypophosphatemia and Rhabdomyolysis in the Setting of a Motor Functional Neurological Disorder
case_prompt:
A 22-year-old athletic man with no other medical history presented with two episodes of acute tetraparesis.

title: Primary perivascular epithelioid cell tumor of the liver: new case report and literature review
case_prompt:

title: Myofibrillar Myopathy Mimicking Polyneuropathy
case_prompt:
A 76-year-old man presented with a 5-year history of progressive leg weakness. He reported right foot slap and tripping on uneven ground, later developing bilateral foot drop, exercise-related leg fatigue, and mild difficulty climbing stairs or rising from a low chair. title: Primary renal leiomyosarcoma: A diagnostic challenge

title: Complete horizontal gaze palsy due to bilateral paramedian pontine reticular formation involvement as a presentation of multiple sclerosis: a case report
case_prompt: A 16-year-old Iranian girl presented with sudden-onset bilateral horizontal gaze palsy that developed overnight. Hyperreflexia was noted in all four limbs without clonus; plantar reflexes were downgoing; muscle strength, tone, and coordination were normal. Brain MRI showed multiple hyperintense subcortical and periventricular lesions on FLAIR and T2-weighted images, and bilateral hyperintensities in the paramedian pontine reticular formation (PPRF) at the level of the pons. Bilateral abducens nucleus lesions would produce similar gaze palsy, but lesions were localized to the PPRF Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“lesions affecting the abducens nuclei will cause ipsilateral horizontal gaze palsy ... bilateral horizontal gaze palsy will be caused if lesion(s) involve both abducens nuclei.Гўв‚¬Вќ 2. MLF lesions (internuclear ophthalmoplegia) were unlikely because they preserve abduction and impair adduction, whereas this patient had complete horizontal gaze loss Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“isolated damage to the MLFs will cause the classic INO where eye abduction is preserved, however, the ipsilateral eye will not be able to be adducted on conjugate horizontal gaze.Гўв‚¬Вќ 3. Infarction or vasculitis were unlikely given imaging features consistent with demyelination and unremarkable laboratory studies Гўв‚¬вЂќ Гўв‚¬Е“This presentation can also have various other etiologies such as infarct, vasculitis, and NMO.Гўв‚¬Вќ

title: Neurolymphomatosis of the brachial plexus from atypical primary central nervous system lymphoma lesions: A case report and review of the literature

title: Acute disseminated encephalomyelitis (ADEM) after SARS- CoV-2 vaccination: A case reportГўЛњвЂ
case_prompt:
